title: Billbergia fastuosa
intro: Billbergia fastuosa, commonly known as the 'Purple Cone Flower' or 'Queen's Tears', is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Billbergia fastuosa include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Billbergia fastuosa is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues and other health conditions.

This family is further divided into several subfamilies, and Bromeliinae is one of the subfamilies to which Billbergia fastuosa belongs. The genus Billbergia itself is a group of flowering plants native to the Americas, with a total of about 55 species. The classification of Billbergia fastuosa provides valuable insights into its evolutionary history and botanical characteristics.
